findings.description: The NC Waste Management / Environmental Quality logo appears in the HTML but the header layout with the governor, secretary, and acting director names is rendered differently as plain text blocks rather than the styled header format.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a formal letterhead with logo on left and officials' names right-aligned in a styled header. HTML shows the same content but with different visual arrangement.

findings.description: The digital signature block appears in the HTML version but is not present in the original PDF page 1.
findings.evidence: HTML page 2 shows 'Digitally signed by Elizabeth S Werner' with DN and date information. The PDF page 1 does not show this digital signature information.

findings.description: In the outline list, the PDF uses Roman numerals with periods as a numbered list format, while the HTML renders them similarly but with slightly different list styling.
findings.evidence: PDF shows I., II., III., IV. as indented list items. HTML shows the same with 'I.', 'II.', 'III.', 'IV.' formatting preserved.

findings.description: The document title 'MEMORANDUM' is rendered as h1 with bold and underline styling, which is acceptable. However, the main sections (I, II, III, IV) are rendered as h2 which is appropriate. No significant hierarchy issues.
findings.evidence: h1: MEMORANDUM, h2: I. History/Background, h2: II. March 16, 2016..., h2: III. Written Public Comment Summary, h2: IV. Attachments — matches PDF structure.

findings.description: The page 1 content (memorandum header, outline, and footer) appears in the HTML as part of the main content, and then the same content effectively appears again as subsequent pages are rendered. The outline (I-IV) appears in the <main> block on page 1 and then the same sections appear as full content below. Additionally, the footer text from page 1 appears both in the <header> area at the top of the document and in a page-footer div.
findings.evidence: The outline list (I. History/Background, II. March 16..., III. Written..., IV. Attachments) appears first in an <ol> and then each section appears again as h2 headings with full content. The footer 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Management / 1646 Mail Service Center...' appears in the page-footer div. This mirrors the PDF structure across pages but creates a nested <main> inside <main> element.

findings.description: There is a nested <main> element inside the outer <main> element, which is invalid HTML structure. The first page content is wrapped in a second <main> tag.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <main> in the <body>, then another <main> tag wrapping the page 1 memorandum content including the outline, creating an invalid nested structure.

findings.description: Minor typo in the source PDF is faithfully preserved: 'creditable' should likely be 'credible', and 'there wells' should be 'their wells' — these are present in both PDF and HTML, so no accuracy issue with the conversion itself.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'no creditable reports' and 'test there wells' — HTML preserves these exactly.
